results.title: Adjournment
results.summary: The Minister for Finance moved the Motion for the 2025 Budget, which is the long-awaited fiscal statement outlining the Government's economic plans and expenditure proposals for the coming year. This debate focuses on the presentation of the Budget rather than specific policy details, as the full financial measures and allocations will be detailed in the accompanying Budget document. No specific policy signals, statistics, or operational constraints were discussed during this introductory segment of the debate.

results.title: Alternative Crossing Designs at Intersections with Heavy Multimodal Traffic
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) pilots alternative crossing designs, including pedestrian scramble crossings, at intersections with high pedestrian demand and limited traffic impact, such as the junction of Robinson Road/Boon Tat Street. Where feasible, existing crossings are widened to accommodate dedicated lanes for pedestrians and active mobility users, as seen at Market Street and East Coast Road. The Minister noted that the scope for implementing such designs is limited. The transcript does not confirm a formal review of existing crossing standards or a specific timeline for future pilots.

results.title: Accounting Treatment of Prolonged Vacancy in HDB-managed Commercial Properties
results.summary: HDB prioritizes sustained occupancy and appropriate tenant mix over maximizing rental collections or maintaining high benchmarks, often awarding tenders to non-highest bidders based on quality factors like affordability and community initiatives. When vacancies arise, HDB assesses the trade-off between reconfiguration works and immediate tendering, aiming to place units for tender as soon as possible to avoid resident inconvenience rather than applying specific vacancy thresholds. The Price-Quality Method is used for coffee shops and supermarkets, where 60% of the score reflects quality metrics. As of 2025, the overall vacancy rate for HDB rental shops stood at 1.6%, reflecting generally low vacancy levels.

results.title: Acknowledgement to the Chair
results.summary: The Leader of the House announced the conclusion of the Budget 2026 process, marking the first budget of the new parliamentary term with a total of $200 billion in estimates. The budget prioritizes harnessing artificial intelligence as a strategic advantage while addressing a historic low total fertility rate of 0.87 through a whole-of-nation effort to support families. Amidst heightened geopolitical tensions and global uncertainty, the government maintains a projected surplus of $15.1 billion for FY2025 to provide fiscal ballast against potential economic shocks. The speech reaffirmed Singapore's unique political model based on trust in leaders' integrity, citing robust debate involving 68 Members and 425 cuts during the supply process.

results.title: Alternative Sports Access Arrangements for Residents Affected by Renovations of ActiveSG Facilities and Centres
results.summary: Sport Singapore informs residents of ActiveSG facility closures in advance via the website and on-site notices, which detail alternative nearby locations including Sport Centres, Sport-in-Precinct facilities, and Dual-Use Scheme facilities. Organised groups and class instructors are engaged early to coordinate displacement plans to other ActiveSG locations. To minimise impact, renovation works are avoided in multiple nearby facilities simultaneously. These measures ensure continued access to sports and community activities during major renovation works at regional or local ActiveSG facilities.

results.title: Applications for Letters of Support from MUIS to Hold Night Prayers during Ramadan at Qaryah Community Group Venues
results.summary: To obtain a Letter of Support from MUIS for holding Tarawih prayers at Qaryah venues, groups must be affiliated with a mosque to receive religious and operational guidance. Applications are submitted directly to MUIS, which mandates that prayers be led by qualified teachers and funds be safeguarded. While historical data for the past decade is unavailable, all applications received in the last three years were successful. The requirement ensures mosques guide Qaryahs on compliance, though specific statistics for non-PA applicants remain untracked in the provided response.

results.title: Assessment of Taiwan's National Lung Cancer Early Detection Programme
results.summary: The Ministry of Health currently recommends low-dose computed tomography (LDCT) lung cancer screening only for current or former heavy smokers in Singapore, excluding the general population. This restriction stems from evidence showing smokers account for approximately 60% of lung cancer cases, while LDCT carries radiation exposure equivalent to over half a year of background levels and a high false-positive rate that triggers unnecessary invasive procedures. Consequently, existing data does not demonstrate that screening benefits for non-smokers outweigh potential health risks from repeated exposure and diagnostic cascades. The Ministry will continue monitoring emerging evidence regarding the suitability of expanding screening to non-smokers as healthcare technology advances.

results.title: Benchmarks to Trigger Review of Rental Rates of HDB Commercial Properties
results.summary: HDB does not define specific vacancy thresholds or duration benchmarks to trigger rental reviews for its commercial properties. Instead, the Board prioritizes sustained occupancy and an appropriate tenant mix over maximizing rental collections, often awarding tenders based on the Price-Quality Method where quality factors like affordability account for 60% of the score. When vacancies arise, HDB assesses the required trade change and conducts reconfiguration works before calling for tenders to optimize the neighbourhood mix. The overall vacancy rate for HDB rental shops remained low at 1.6% in 2025, with units released for tender as soon as possible to minimize resident inconvenience.

results.title: Bicycle Parking Capacity Standards in HDB Estates
results.summary: Since 2014, HDB provides one dedicated bicycle lot per six dwelling units in new Build-To-Order projects, with pre-2014 estates receiving this provision since 2019 where feasible. Additional safeguarded space exists for conversion by Town Councils to meet demand, and HDB collaborates with them to install racks in void decks. Regarding bike-sharing, the Land Transport Authority is currently reviewing implementation of additional designated spaces for shared bicycle parking in HDB estates.

results.title: Breakdown of Defence Contracts Procurement by Quantum and Procurement Method and Publication of Requirements for Major Platform Replacements on GeBIZ
results.summary: The Ministry of Defence (MINDEF) prioritizes open tenders via GeBIZ but utilizes closed tenders when justified by operational security, intellectual property, or system interoperability. While local firms are invited to participate in closed tenders if they possess relevant expertise, MINDEF does not publicly disclose specific contract data or quantities due to security constraints. Consequently, the Minister declined to publish a breakdown of contracts above $10 million or $100 million procured through closed versus open methods. The Ministry maintains that its procurement processes adhere to strict governance, audits, and transparency principles despite these security-driven limitations on public disclosure.

results.title: Cases where Salaries of Public Sector Employees Are Not Paid On Time
results.summary: In 2025, there were 35 cases of late salary payments for public sector employees, representing approximately 0.02% of over 158,000 officers. These instances involved new public officers experiencing administrative delays in updating appointment records within the payroll system, which were immediately rectified with salaries paid in the subsequent month. The government attributes potential future delays to system errors or outages, committing to ensure affected officers receive payment in the next payroll cycle. Affected employees may raise concerns with their human resource department for investigation and rectification, while the Public Service Division and Accountant-General's Department continuously monitor and enhance payroll systems to maintain timely payment schedules.

results.title: Civil List
results.summary: This Parliament resolves to vary the Schedule to the Civil List and Gratuity Act 1970, effective from FY2025, by increasing Class 2, 3, and 4 expenditure allocations to $7,318,000, $4,542,000, and $1,288,000 respectively. The motion explicitly maintains the President's salary and allowance under Class 1 while adjusting other categories to reflect rising operating costs, IT security expenses, Istana restoration works, and the re-categorisation of seconded public officers from Class 2 to Class 4. These changes address increased manpower and household costs without altering the President's personal remuneration.

results.title: Clarification by Minister for Sustainability and the Environment
results.summary: Minister for Sustainability and the Environment Grace Fu Hai Yen issued a factual correction regarding "Transiently Floodable Areas (Coastal)" under the Coastal Protection and Other Amendments Bill. Previously stated that these areas need not be protected against permanent flooding but need to be protected against transient coastal flooding, the Minister clarified that they must be protected against permanent flooding but need not be protected against transient coastal flooding. This correction applies to Prescribed Places bounded by an Absolute Protection Boundary on the landward side and a Protection Boundary on the seaward side. The amendment addresses the specific protection requirements for these designated coastal zones.

results.title: Committee of Supply – Total Sums for Main Estimates, Development Estimates and Resolutions
results.summary: The Committee of Supply has concluded its deliberations and adopted specific resolutions regarding the Main Estimates, Development Estimates, and related resolutions. Ms Indranee Rajah moved for Parliament to agree to these resolutions, effectively finalizing the supply committee's recommendations for the current fiscal year. No further amendments or additional measures were proposed during this stage of the proceedings. The House is now positioned to proceed with the formal approval of the agreed-upon financial estimates.

results.title: Community Feedback Regarding Festival Naming Conventions and Cultural Greetings
results.summary: Agencies under the Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth receive occasional community feedback regarding festival naming conventions, such as "Chinese New Year" versus "Lunar New Year" or "Hari Raya" versus "Eid." The Acting Minister noted that public holiday names are well-established, though individuals may use alternative terms based on context and audience. The official approach encourages all parties to make greetings in the spirit of respect and inclusivity. No specific timeline or mandatory standardization was announced for these naming preferences.

results.title: Compliance with National Childhood and Adolescent Immunisation Schedule and Actions Taken Against Non-compliance in Past 10 Years
results.summary: Under the National Childhood Immunisation Schedule, measles and diphtheria vaccinations are compulsory for children aged 12 and below residing in Singapore. In 2024, measles vaccination coverage reached 93% at two years and 97% at seven years, while diphtheria coverage was 89% at two years and 95% at eleven years. Foreign students aged 12 and below must submit documented proof of vaccination or immunity to the National Immunisation Registry before applying for long-term immigration passes. The Minister stated that these measures, combined with cooperation from families, have maintained herd immunity against measles and diphtheria over the past decade.

results.title: Data on ASEAN Scholarship Holders' Acquisition of Singapore Citizenship and Permanent Residency Since 1998
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that ASEAN Scholarship holders may acquire Permanent Residency or Citizenship progressively from school through later life, with recent cohorts showing about 70% becoming residents or citizens within five years of graduation. The Ministry notes that many recipients stay to complete university studies before making this decision, while others contribute to the economy after their bond ends or join Singapore's global network. Specific aggregate numbers for total holders since 1998 or the exact percentage of the total cohort were not provided in the response.

results.title: Data on Nationality, Race and Educational Level for Students Granted ASEAN Scholarships
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) awards an average of 800 ASEAN scholarships annually, comprising over 400 pre-tertiary and 400 tertiary scholarships since 1998. Recipients may remain in Singapore post-graduation or maintain global ties. The Minister stated that data on scholarship holders is not published by nationality, race, or educational stage. Consequently, specific breakdowns for these demographic categories are unavailable.

results.title: Digital Support Provided to Help Heritage Businesses Modernise and Sustain Their Operations
results.summary: The National Heritage Board administers the SG Heritage Business Scheme, offering designated heritage businesses support for brand visibility, publicity, and marketing through special branding markers and a dedicated toolkit. Eligible businesses can access the Organisation Transformation Grant to fund transformative digital projects, such as Augmented Reality applications and online games, enhancing operational sustainability and long-term viability. Furthermore, heritage enterprises may utilise Enterprise Singapore's Productivity Solutions Grant, Enterprise Development Grant, and Market Readiness Assistance Grant to adopt digital solutions and expand their local and international presence. In the coming months, the NHB plans to launch a promotional campaign, establish an online register of designated businesses, and identify industry partners for collaboration to further support these initiatives.

results.title: Evaluation Criteria for Tenders Submitted by Prospective Socially-conscious Enterprise Hawker Centre Operators
results.summary: The National Environment Agency publishes the full evaluation criteria and respective weightages for Socially-conscious Enterprise Hawker Centre (SEHC) management tenders within the tender documents available on the Government Electronic Business portal. These documents are accessible to prospective operators seeking to understand the specific requirements and scoring mechanisms applied by the agency. No additional disclosure of internal deliberations or unlisted metrics is indicated in the response.

results.title: Expanding Use of ActiveSG Credits to Include Sports Facilities Such As Those at Community Clubs and The Kallang
results.summary: The Government is committed to ensuring public sports facilities remain accessible and affordable. ActiveSG credits are currently usable at designated ActiveSG facilities, including the World Aquatics Championship Arena at The Kallang, specific community sport hubs, and over 370 school sports facilities under the Dual-Use Scheme since November 2025. While the Ministry has not yet expanded credit usage to all community clubs, SportSG is actively enhancing the My ActiveSG+ booking system and plans to explore expanding credit acceptance at People's Association facilities in the future. This initiative aims to remove barriers and encourage wider participation in sports activities across Singapore.

results.title: Expansion of HDB Flyer Distribution Rules at HDB Homes as Mandatory Industry Regulation
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the agreement between the top five estate agencies to ensure HDB flyer visibility is restricted to a voluntary memorandum of understanding. This arrangement does not extend to a mandatory regulation for all Council for Estate Agencies registered agents. The specific question regarding the extension of these rules to become mandatory industry regulation was not addressed in the Ministry's reply dated 4 March 2026.

results.title: Export Potential of Singapore’s Water Recycling and Management Capabilities and Support for Water Technology Firms to Compete Globally
results.summary: The Government has invested approximately $800 million in Research, Innovation and Enterprise (RIE) since 2006, expanding the water technology sector from 50 to roughly 400 companies and creating over 20,000 jobs. Agencies like Enterprise Singapore, PUB, and the Singapore Water Association support local firms such as Keppel and Sembcorp through initiatives including the biennial Singapore International Water Week, business missions, and grants like the Market Readiness Assistance grant and Enhanced Enterprise Development Grant. PUB has opened its facilities for export-oriented test bedding via the Living Lab fund to validate technologies for industrial and municipal needs. There are no immediate plans to establish a circular economy export office or introduce sovereign project bonds, though existing schemes are enhanced to assist companies in securing international contracts.

results.title: Committee of Supply – Head I (Ministry of Social and Family Development)
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF) is advancing its "3S" outcomes of stability, self-reliance, and social mobility through three pillars: a safety net, steadying hand, and supported ladder. To address slowing progress for lower-income families, MSF is enhancing ComLink+ Progress Packages with targeted support across income security, housing, and health, while expanding pilots for social health integration and skills upgrading via family coaches. Approximately 250 Special Education graduates transition to employment or further studies within six months, reflecting ongoing efforts to build lifelong security for persons with disabilities. MSF is also refining support models to ensure active community participation, improving preschool workforce conditions, and addressing digital safety for children, though specific timelines for full per-child funding extension and detailed data-sharing protocols remain under development.

results.title: Facilitating Adoption of AI in Energy Generation Sector
results.summary: In 2025, the Energy Market Authority launched a regulatory sandbox for Virtual Power Plants, enabling AI-driven optimization of distributed energy resources like solar panels and batteries. The Government supports energy efficiency through Enterprise Singapore's Cost and Carbon Reduction Programme, which utilizes a proprietary AI tool to help SMEs identify decarbonisation opportunities. To address workforce needs, SkillsFuture Singapore and partners developed a Skills Framework for Energy and Power mapping digital competencies, with plans to update curricula for niche skills. Officials confirmed that Singapore is studying international AI use cases for predictive maintenance and operational efficiency in energy generation and grid networks to inform local adoption strategies.

results.title: Factors for Decisions on When and Where to Deploy Crow-shooting Operations
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the criteria for resuming crow-shooting operations, officer selection protocols, comparative effectiveness of methods, and expected operational frequency were previously addressed in a Ministry reply dated 5 March 2026. No new details regarding deployment conditions, safety measures, or population reduction timelines were provided during this session. Consequently, the specific answers to the four sub-questions remain unchanged from the earlier response. Users seeking updated operational parameters should refer to the original 5 March 2026 parliamentary record for the full policy context.

results.title: Feedback and Compensation Claims Made by Yio Chu Kang SMC Residents on Damages from North-South Corridor Construction Along Ang Mo Kio Avenue 6
results.summary: Over the past 24 months, the Land Transport Authority received 19 damage claims from private residential property owners in Yio Chu Kang SMC related to North-South Corridor construction along Ang Mo Kio Avenue 6. Investigations determined that 18 cases were not caused by the works, while one recent case remains under assessment. Resolution timelines vary based on case complexity and the requirement for detailed site inspections. No specific service standards or compensation amounts were stated in the response.

results.title: Frameworks for Stress-testing Impact on Singapore's Economy When Major Trading Partners Make Sudden Legal and Policy Decisions
results.summary: The Singapore Economic Resilience Taskforce (SERT), established in April 2025, monitors macroeconomic indicators including GDP, exports, and unemployment to assess impacts from sudden policy shifts in major trading partners. To support trade-exposed sectors, the Government prioritizes market diversification, deepening investment linkages with like-minded partners in digital and green economies, and upholding a rules-based trading system. The Business Adaptation Grant, launched in October 2025, assists eligible enterprises in adapting operations and strengthening supply chains with support levels of 50%, enhanced to 70% at Budget 2026. SERT continues gathering feedback from tripartite and industry partners to inform contingency planning for businesses and workers facing global economic uncertainties.

results.title: Guidelines for Teachers and Schools on Handling of Students with SEN
results.summary: The Ministry of Education provides standardised guidelines, video resources, and practical strategies via Teaching and Learning Guides to support students with special educational needs (SEN). All new teachers receive basic SEN training during pre-service, while existing staff deepen skills through optional online modules on the SEN:se platform, the SEN Resource Portal, and the Transition Support Framework. Schools employ Teachers Trained in Special Needs who hold a 130-hour certificate-level course and SEN Officers to develop customised interventions. While the Ministry facilitates systematic sharing of best practices and reviews guidelines against international and local research, it does not specify a mandatory minimum number of SEN-related training hours for continuous professional development, leaving teachers flexibility to select relevant modules. Separate information is also provided to parents via the Parents Gateway to support their children with SEN.

results.title: Coastal Protection and Other Amendments Bill
results.summary: The Coastal Protection and Other Amendments Bill designates PUB as the national coastal protection agency under the amended Public Utilities Act, while renaming the Sewerage and Drainage Act to the Sewerage, Drainage and Coastal Protection Act. To optimize scarce land, coastal protection responsibilities are decentralized to landowners of "Prescribed Places," with PUB coordinating island-wide works to ensure measures remain connected and water-tight. A capital grant framework covers study, diversion, and construction costs in reimbursement tranches, while centralised schemes like the Greater Southern Waterfront barriers will be fully funded by the Government without cost recovery from landowners. Liability for water-tightness failures rests with the executing landowner, and subsequent owners generally follow standard property transfer principles regarding responsibilities.

results.title: Impact of Indonesia's Purchase of KHAN Ballistic Missile on Regional Security
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Public Services and Minister for Defence stated that the Singapore Armed Forces continuously monitors security developments, including emerging technologies like ballistic missiles. He affirmed that the SAF has and will continue to build up defence capabilities ahead of time to address threats. The Minister did not provide a specific assessment of Indonesia's KHAN missile acquisition or detail the basis for Singapore's adequacy against such threats. No further elaboration on the threat environment or specific operational constraints was given in the response.

results.title: Implementation of Fire Prevention Measures for Elderly Residents in Mature Estates
results.summary: The Ministry for Home Affairs and SCDF address fire risks for elderly residents in mature estates through mandatory Home Fire Alarm Devices (HFADs) since 2018 and expanded subsidies under the Enhancement for Active Seniors (EASE) programme, offering up to 95% coverage for installation. The Housing and Development Board mitigates electrical fault risks by checking and replacing faulty Residual Current Circuit Breakers in older flats via the Home Improvement Programme and EASE. While SCDF continues to assess suitable technological solutions for seniors, the Ministry emphasizes that homeowners bear the primary responsibility for fire prevention. Awareness campaigns, including the Responders Plus Programme and MyNicehome Roadshows, are ongoing to educate residents on unsafe practices. The Ministry will incorporate new technological solutions into homes where deemed suitable.

results.title: Inclusion of Darker Colours in Solar Reflectance Standards for Cool Coatings under Green Towns Programme
results.summary: Under the HDB Green Towns Programme, cool coatings must comply with SS678, currently offering over 60 colours. While darker shades like red brick may be precluded if they fail to meet solar reflectance standards, suppliers may progressively expand colour selection based on market demand. Town Councils can verify availability of compliant darker colours with paint suppliers. HDB will continue sharing operational guidelines and good practices with Town Councils to assist in managing resident aesthetic preferences against technical requirements.

results.title: Lighting Designs for Shared Paths to Ensure Adequate Night-time Lighting for Tree Canopy Growth and Differentiation at Zones with Heavier Pedestrian and Active Mobility Traffic
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that lighting at potential conflict areas on cycling paths, such as sharp bends and intersections, must be twice the brightness of other paths. Operational guidelines ensure plantings do not obstruct lighting, while features like "SLOW" markings and speed-regulating strips are used to manage speed in high-traffic zones. These measures balance adequate night-time illumination with the need to maintain tree canopy and shade for daytime active mobility. Specific lighting standards are differentiated for zones with heavier pedestrian and active mobility traffic to address safety and growth requirements.

results.title: Mobile Network Dead Zones in MRT Network and Steps to Rectify Persistent Signal Gaps
results.summary: IMDA requires Mobile Network Operators to maintain over 99% mobile coverage in all MRT tunnels and stations, publishing quarterly Quality of Service results on its website. When coverage gaps are identified, IMDA collaborates with operators to implement enhancements, a process typically completed within six months using limited maintenance windows. Over the past three years, no persistent signal gaps within the MRT network have remained unrectified beyond this timeframe. The Minister confirmed that no persistent gaps currently exist, though specific financial penalties for non-compliance or tracking of operator-specific non-compliance records for future spectrum allocation were not addressed in the response.

results.title: More Details in HIP Renovation Schedules
results.summary: The Minister stated that HDB provides estimated Home Improvement Programme (HIP) renovation schedules for each block on the precinct website and notice boards at least one month before works commence at the first block. Residents receive reminders two weeks and three days prior to commencement. The exact timing depends on factors including the progress of preceding blocks, site-specific complexities, contractor survey times, and resident selection of optional items. While HDB aims to provide schedules as early as possible, providing them well in advance remains challenging due to these variables. Residents may approach the HIP Information Centre for enquiries regarding their specific schedules.

results.title: National Servicemen Who Have Applied for Redeployment under Enhanced Expertise Deployment Scheme and Work-learn Schemes
results.summary: The Enhanced Expertise Deployment Scheme, introduced in 2022, allows Operationally Ready National Servicemen (NSmen) with specialised civilian expertise to redeploy from existing roles to support the Singapore Armed Forces in domains like Medical, Legal, Digital, Cyber, and Communications. Approximately 1,000 NSmen indicated interest annually over the past three years, with about 20% successfully redeployed after selection processes match their skillsets to available openings. The Coordinating Minister noted that numbers have not yet stabilised as the scheme is new. The Work-Learn Scheme is distinct, involving a combination of full-time National Service followed by Regular service rather than redeployment. Specific criteria or skill gaps limiting redeployment were not explicitly detailed in the response.

results.title: Parking Management Companies Selling VPCs Without Designated Lots in Last Three Years and Enforcement Measures Taken
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority is reviewing the Vehicle Parking Certificate (VPC) scheme and intends to investigate and strengthen enforcement against errant heavy vehicle parking operators selling VPCs without designated lots. The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed these actions in response to inquiries regarding cases in the last three years and potential alternative parking proof mechanisms. Specific case counts and a definitive timeline for the review or enforcement tightening were not provided in the statement.

results.title: Progress and Budget Updates on AquaPolis Programme
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency, National University of Singapore, and Temasek Life Sciences Laboratory established AquaPolis to drive innovation and talent development for the aquaculture sector. The programme secured an $18.5 million budget for a three-year R&D initiative starting November 2025, aiming to lower production costs and improve the quality of locally produced Asian seabass. While AquaPolis launched an industry consortium platform to facilitate knowledge sharing and translate research outcomes into practical solutions, the Minister did not explicitly confirm whether the specific pledge to support fingerling supply has been delivered or provide a timeline for such delivery.

results.title: Proposal to Establish Master Database of Commercial Sublet Agreements
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the proposal to establish a master database of commercial sublet agreements and to mandate lodging these agreements with HDB as a tenancy condition was already addressed in a previous reply dated 2 March 2026. No new measures, timelines, or operational changes regarding this specific request were announced during the current session. The Ministry indicated that the matter has been previously handled, and no further action on these specific proposals is currently being considered.

results.title: Ratification Plans of Arms Trade Treaty and Usage of Singapore-Origin Conventional Arms in International Crimes
results.summary: Singapore has not yet ratified the Arms Trade Treaty (ATT) but remains a signatory committed to regulating international arms trade and preventing illicit diversion. The Government ensures necessary legal, policy, and institutional frameworks are established before ratification, noting that only the Philippines among ASEAN members has ratified the treaty so far. Singapore actively contributes to international initiatives, including the UN Programme of Action and the International Tracing Instrument, while strictly complying with international obligations on arms sales and UN sanctions. Regular reports submitted to the UN Register of Conventional Arms since 1993 are publicly available, and the Government will take action against any entity contravening its export control regime.

results.title: Real-Time Information Sharing Protocols with Foreign Authorities on Child Trafficking Syndicates Linked to Singapore Adoptions
results.summary: The Singapore Police Force (SPF) maintains real-time information sharing protocols with foreign authorities, including Interpol, regarding transnational child trafficking. Following a September 2025 request from the Indonesian National Police (INP) concerning alleged baby trafficking for adoption, the SPF is currently assisting INP investigations. No other joint operations or information requests related to child trafficking syndicates linked to Singapore adoptions occurred in the preceding five years. For updates on potential enhancements to upstream efforts like pre-clearance checks, the Ministry of Social and Family Development issued a reply on 3 February 2026.

results.title: Reports of Unauthorised Works on LTA-managed State Land Fronting Private Properties in Past Three Years and Policy on Cost Liability of Restoration Works
results.summary: In the past three years, the Land Transport Authority received approximately 25 reports of unauthorised works on road reserves fronting private landed housing, with four cases rectified by owners. Remaining cases assessed as not affecting safety and connectivity are pending resolution with homeowners. Where unauthorised works on public roads or footpaths compromise safety or connectivity, property owners must immediately restore the affected areas at their own cost.

results.title: Singapore Citizens with Additional Hanyu Pinyin Name Registered as Part of Legal Name
results.summary: As of February 2026, approximately 13% of Singapore Citizens have registered an additional Hanyu Pinyin name with the Immigration and Checkpoints Authority. Between 2016 and 2025, an average of about 3,500 Singapore Citizen babies annually had this additional name included during birth registration. The Minister provided these statistics in response to a parliamentary question regarding the prevalence and trends of Hanyu Pinyin name registrations among citizens. No further details on eligibility criteria or future policy changes were stated in the transcript.

results.title: Singapore Dollar-specific Factors Contributing to Persistent Weakness against Malaysian Ringgit
results.summary: The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) manages the Singapore dollar against a trade-weighted currency basket rather than a bilateral rate, noting that non-resident labour flows reflect overall economic growth. While the Singapore dollar has appreciated 1.3% annually against the Malaysian ringgit since 2020, MAS will adjust monetary policy to ensure medium-term price stability. Regarding the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link, which will enhance cross-border travel by year-end, the Ministry of Trade and Industry (MTI) is addressing potential impacts on local retailers through the RTS Link Task Force. Enhancements to grants for heartland businesses have been announced in the Committee of Supply, with further measures to support merchant competitiveness expected to be detailed soon.

results.title: Statistics on Do Not Call Complaints, Investigations and Fines for Past Two Years, and Measures Taken to Improve DNC Registry Enforcement and Compliance
results.summary: The Personal Data Protection Commission (PDPC) enforces Do Not Call (DNC) provisions through complaint investigations and business engagement. DNC complaints declined after the 2021 regime update, with approximately 1,700 received in 2024 and 1,380 in 2025. Of these, 122 cases in 2024 and 176 in 2025 were investigated for potential breaches. None of the investigated cases warranted financial penalties. The Minister noted that the PDPC continues to monitor industry practices to ensure compliance with DNC regulations.

results.title: Supplementary Supply (FY2025) Bill
results.summary: The Supplementary Supply (FY2025) Bill seeks to authorize additional expenditure exceeding the Supply Act 2025 limits for the financial year 1 April 2025 to 31 March 2026. These extra sums were previously approved by the House via the Supplementary Estimates of Expenditure (Paper Cmd No 5 of 2026) under Articles 148(2) and 148C of the Constitution. The Bill proceeds to its Second and Third readings to enact this provision. No further policy details or specific expenditure categories were disclosed in the transcript.

results.title: Supply Bill
results.summary: The Supply Bill seeks legislative approval for appropriations from the Consolidated Fund and Development Fund to cover expenditure for the financial year 1 April 2026 to 31 March 2027. Heads of Expenditure and corresponding sums are detailed in the Bill's Schedule, having been previously approved by the House in the Main and Development Estimates of Expenditure (Paper Cmd No 4 of 2026). Upon approval, the Bill will empower the Minister to issue warrants authorising payments up to the specified amounts for each Head. The Bill is currently proceeding to its Third Reading.

results.title: Training on Special Needs Recognition and Response in Mandatory WSQ Modules for Licensed Security Officers
results.summary: Currently, mandatory Workforce Skills Qualifications (WSQ) modules under the Progressive Wage Model for licensed security officers do not include training on recognising and responding to persons with special needs. These modules focus on core skills like threat detection and incident management to ensure premises security. The Government will continue to review training requirements in consultation with the industry to ensure officers can carry out their duties effectively. No specific timeline for adding this training was stated.

results.title: Tuberculosis Patients Who Have sought Financial Assistance to Cover Travel Expenses and Lost Income
results.summary: Tuberculosis patients in Singapore can seek financial assistance for travel expenses and lost income related to Directly Observed Therapy (DOT) via Medical Social Workers at the National Tuberculosis Care Centre and polyclinics. Eligible residents may access government schemes including MediFund, ComCare's Short-to-Medium-Term Assistance, and the Agency for Integrated Care's long-term care scheme. Additionally, the Singapore Anti-Tuberculosis Association CommHealth offers grocery vouchers and transport allowances through its DOT and Shop scheme to promote adherence. TB treatment is heavily subsidised for residents, with a progressive shift toward risk-based approaches allowing good adherers to transition to Video Observed Therapy or reduced DOT frequency.

results.title: Usage of Data Collected from i-SMART Facial Recognition at NESST Tukang Dormitory
results.summary: The i-SMART facial recognition system at NESST Tukang dormitory collects residents' facial data, entry/exit timestamps, and body temperature readings solely for access control and health monitoring. This personal information is used exclusively by NESST Singapore Limited, the dormitory operator, and is not shared with the Ministry or employers. Data sharing with relevant authorities occurs only when necessary, such as during disease outbreaks, in accordance with applicable laws.

results.title: Wait Times for Pedestrians at Signalised Junctions
results.summary: The median and maximum pedestrian wait times at signalised junctions are 120 and 140 seconds respectively, though Friendly Streets junctions achieve waits under 60 seconds by 2030. While the Land Transport Authority prioritises mass transport and active mobility, signal timing balances pedestrian needs with traffic flow, including public bus schedules. Dynamic systems currently track traffic volume, with plans to install cameras at pedestrian waiting areas to incorporate pedestrian data for dynamic adjustments within a specific range. The green man button activates crossings based on demand, functioning faster at some junctions while others rely on low pedestrian volume to maintain traffic flow. Future optimisation will utilise Electronic Road Pricing (ERP) 2.0 data to better assess needs and achieve an optimal balance for all road users.

results.title: Wider Use for SG Culture Pass for Purchasing SingLit Books
results.summary: From 1 March 2026, the Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth (MCCY) expanded the SG Culture Pass to allow Singaporeans to use credits for purchasing physical Singapore literature (SingLit) from participating bookstores. Eligible titles include fiction, poetry, plays, and short essays in English, Chinese, Malay, and Tamil, provided the author was a Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident at publication. MCCY confirmed there are no plans to introduce a separate "Literary Pass" or specific credits for SingLit, as the existing Culture Pass scheme already covers this purpose. The ministry will review feedback from this initial phase before considering future expansions to other literary works purchasable with Culture Pass credits.

results.title: Tobacco (Control of Advertisements and Sale) (Amendment) and Other Matters Bill
results.summary: The Government will rename the Tobacco (Control of Advertisements and Sale) Act to the Tobacco and Vaporisers Control Act (TVCA) to establish a structured legal framework replacing temporary measures. This new regime prohibits vaporisers, tobacco products, and imitation tobacco products while introducing a category for "Specified Psychoactive Substances" (SPS), with etomidate reclassified from the Misuse of Drugs Act to the TVCA. The Bill addresses enforcement capacity, ensuring HSA officers undergo rigorous training and competency assessments before deployment, and confirms sufficient rehabilitation capacity exists within existing Drug Rehabilitation Centres. Operational clarity is provided regarding the distinction between regulated substances under the TVCA and those under the Misuse of Drugs Act, alongside provisions for upstream disruption of supply networks and expanded smoking bans in public places.
